title: Chamber Of Potions
name: Harini Sivakumar
text: Oh man, the food and drinks here are straight-up magical! I can't recommend the green apple martini and Long Island Iced Tea enough—they're pure bliss in a glass, bursting with flavor that hits you right in the soul. Most places nail either the drinks or the food, but here? Both absolutely rock and leave you craving more. And Manoj's service? Outstanding doesn't even cover it—it's warm, attentive, and makes you feel like royalty!

title: Chamber Of Potions
name: Bhuvana Karna
text: I went along with my friends on Sunday. There were very few people even for a weekend. We ordered shots and starters. Shots were priced too much. Food also too pricy and the portion is extremely low. They also didn't have any veg or fruit salads and cheese. Everything was too oily and taste also very very okyish. On the whole we don't want to go there again as it is not worthy enough

title: Chamber Of Potions
name: prakash raj
text: It was truly a wonderful night filled with great food and lots of fun. As a team, we chose Chambers of Potions, and it exceeded our expectations in every way. The variety of dishes was impressive, and each item was packed with flavor. We especially loved the chicken paniyaaram, which was absolutely top-notch, along with the delicious chicken ghee roast, coin parotta, and cheese balls. A heartfelt special thanks to Chef Dhilip and Manoj for their exceptional culinary skills and warm hospitality that made our experience truly memorable.

title: Chamber Of Potions
name: KaRtHiKeYaN S
text: Mano has good hospitality skills and taken care very well

title: Chamber Of Potions
name: Ram Kumar
text:
What an amazing experience! The food, the ambience, the DJ—everything was just awesome. The Sambar Idli, Ghee Chilli Chicken and Coin Paratha were absolutely extraordinary and easily the highlights of the evening.

A special shoutout to Chef Dilip for making our date truly memorable—something we’ll cherish for ages. One of the finest evenings we’ve had. Highly recommended!

title: Chamber Of Potions
name: Âbishek Sankar
text:
They had lot of drink and food options, we are more foody than alcoholics, so we went for few LIIT and mocktails.
Food was really great, Garlic butter prawn and prawn tempura was too Good, also good options in beef too, fire cracker lotus stem was great too.
We also enjoyed vodka Pani Puri and body mary sea food spaghetti which had an enormous quantity and delicious too.
Ended our main course with awesome chicken tikka Pizza and chicken parota which was almost like kizhi parota.
After such a spread unfortunately we didn't have space for desserts. The kitchen closes by 10:30, the portions of the foods were bit unpredictable, some are overloaded, some are small sized bites, so we ended up ordering more than we could eat. But overall had an wonderful experience, thanks to Mano, he helped us with food selections and also explained about portions later when we asked him. Calm Third floor vibe feels great than Fourth floor vibe, luckily we got the table at the end on Fourth floor, so it wasn't loud and we had a great time.

title: Chamber Of Potions
name: Nirupama Sriram
text:
We tried this interesting restaurant for dinner . The ambience was great. We were led to comfortable seats by Manoj. The service was fast and the food delicious. We loved the starters and the dessert was heavenly.
Manoj took very good care of us till the end.
Altogether a very nice experience. Thank you Chamber of potions.

title: Chamber Of Potions
name: Karthick S
text:
Mehhh experience
First we went to the forth floor , the DJ was playing music and the only table left for us was right next to his speakers. Absolutely not no one in their right mind would sit there.
So we went to calm vibes of the third floor, the cocktails were great, beer was good but the food was average given the price we paid.
The best part of my experience was when I asked for pepper as the pepper grinder in my table didn’t work, as a matter of fact none of them worked throughout the restaurant as waiter boys went and examined every table, isn’t the fundamental rule of setting up a table in a restaurant is to have salt and pepper for the guest ???? even road side shops have them … all that fancy bs but still fail at a fundamental level. Then the boys gave me a cup of crushed pepper 👏🏽👏🏽
So many options on the menu for desserts yet they serve only one with ice cream and the only flavor of ice cream available was vanilla…. Please don’t the make the annoyed with silly things and have everything that is written on the menu

name: Deepak Khadka
text: Yesterday I visited chamber of  portion with    my family. I went to many bars but among them, I liked this restaurant the most. The food was impressive especially , pepper chicken paniyaram , malaka style chicken , corn cheese ball ,  nasi goreng  patter , zucchini chips which were stand out , super crunchy & flavour. They have wich own cocktail which I really liked and the flavor was also good.  Overall my family absolutely love the experience . A hug thanks to our service boy mr.gopal & salim who was very helpfull & professional.

text: A wonderful experience.came for a friend's birthday party and the treatment was amazing.They even surprised us with a watermelon cake which was so unexpected.. I absolutely adore the waiters and staffs for their kindness and will be returning soon. Drinks were creative with a bold taste,the bartenders really did a great job crafting the drinks which made our experience even more elevated..Foods were super tasty with variety of options for both veg and non veg.. Everything is 10/10,a must place to visit if u still haven't visited yet.

Definitely one of the best restobars around .

title: Chamber Of Potions
name: Sumanto Bhandari
text:
⭐ 0/5 – Never Again!

Visited Chamber of Potions (CoP) at Anna Nagar, and honestly, it was one of the most disappointing experiences I’ve ever had. After the first round of drinks, the staff clearly assumed we couldn’t tell the difference between cheap liquor and a proper single malt – big mistake.

The food? Stale and tasteless. If the veg dishes were that bad, I didn’t dare try the non-veg. Hygiene was a total disaster — I actually saw someone poke their nose while preparing a drink. Yes, you read that right. Disgusting.

And to top it all off, they had the audacity to slap on a 10% service charge — maybe that’s for the “organic addition” to the drinks.

A local wine shop would’ve been a better experience. Avoid this place unless you’re into stale food, fake liquor, and questionable “garnishes.” Never going back here again.
